freeze_rule: Frozen after Tier 4.30g-hw passed returned EBRAINS hardware ingest with lifecycle telemetry, predeclared lifecycle sham controls, resource/readback accounting, and one bounded useful task effect: enabled lifecycle opened the task gate while five controls closed it.
strongest_claim: CRA has a bounded lifecycle-native baseline on real SpiNNaker: static-pool lifecycle state, lineage/active-mask/trophic counters, multi-core lifecycle profile isolation, lifecycle sham controls, and a host-ferried lifecycle-to-task bridge have all passed canonical gates. Enabled lifecycle opens the bounded task gate; five predeclared controls close it; resource/readback accounting is returned and preserved.
claim_boundaries: Lifecycle-native baseline, not full organism autonomy.
Tier 4.30g-hw is a host-ferried lifecycle task-benefit/resource bridge; it is not autonomous lifecycle-to-learning MCPL.
Static-pool lifecycle only; no dynamic PyNN population creation and no multi-chip scaling claim.
Not speedup evidence and not proof that all software v2.2 temporal mechanisms are native on chip.
Does not prove external-baseline superiority, language, planning, AGI, ASI, or long-horizon autonomous behavior.
Host still owns setup, package execution, selected bridge ferrying, and evidence readback.

canonical_evidence_entries.plan_position: Phase E lifecycle-native path selection before implementation
canonical_evidence_entries.claim: Initial lifecycle-native work should layer on CRA_NATIVE_MECHANISM_BRIDGE_v0.3 with v2.2 as software reference only, using static-pool lifecycle constraints.
canonical_evidence_entries.caveat: Engineering audit only; not lifecycle implementation, not hardware evidence, not speedup, not multi-chip scaling, and not native v2.2 temporal migration.

canonical_evidence_entries.plan_position: Phase E lifecycle-native contract before reference/runtime work
canonical_evidence_entries.claim: Tier 4.30 defines the lifecycle init/event/trophic/readback/sham command schema, readback fields, event semantics, gate sequence, and failure classes for static-pool lifecycle migration.
canonical_evidence_entries.caveat: Local engineering contract only; not runtime implementation, not hardware evidence, not lifecycle/self-scaling proof, and not v2.2 temporal migration.

canonical_evidence_entries.plan_position: Phase E lifecycle-native deterministic local reference
canonical_evidence_entries.claim: The Tier 4.30 static-pool lifecycle contract has deterministic 8-slot/2-founder reference traces with exact active-mask, lineage, event-count, checksum, and sham-control outputs.
canonical_evidence_entries.caveat: Local deterministic reference only; not runtime C, not hardware evidence, not task benefit, not lifecycle baseline freeze, and not v2.2 temporal-state migration.

canonical_evidence_entries.plan_position: Phase E lifecycle-native runtime source/schema gate before hardware smoke
canonical_evidence_entries.claim: The lifecycle static-pool state surface exists in the custom runtime, matches Tier 4.30a reference checksums in host tests, preserves existing profile/readback tests, and is ready for a single-core hardware mask/lineage smoke.
canonical_evidence_entries.caveat: Local source/runtime host evidence only; not hardware evidence, not task-effect evidence, not multi-core lifecycle migration, and not a baseline freeze.

canonical_evidence_entries.tier_label: Tier 4.30b-hw - Single-Core Lifecycle Active-Mask/Lineage Hardware Smoke
canonical_evidence_entries.plan_position: Phase E lifecycle-native single-core hardware smoke
canonical_evidence_entries.claim: The lifecycle static-pool metadata surface executed on real SpiNNaker with compact lifecycle readback and exact canonical/boundary state parity after correcting a known runner criterion defect.
canonical_evidence_entries.caveat: Hardware smoke only; not lifecycle task benefit, not multi-core lifecycle migration, not speedup, not multi-chip scaling, and not a lifecycle baseline freeze.

canonical_evidence_entries.caveat: Local contract/reference evidence only; not C runtime implementation, not EBRAINS hardware evidence, not lifecycle task benefit, and not a lifecycle baseline freeze.

test_results.name: Tier 4.30d - Multi-Core Lifecycle Runtime Source Audit

canonical_evidence_entries.plan_position: Phase E lifecycle-native five-profile hardware smoke before sham-control subset
canonical_evidence_entries.claim: The five-profile lifecycle runtime surface built, loaded, and executed on real SpiNNaker with profile ownership guards, duplicate/stale lifecycle rejection, and exact canonical/boundary lifecycle parity.
canonical_evidence_entries.caveat: Hardware smoke only; not lifecycle task benefit, not lifecycle sham-control success, not speedup, not multi-chip scaling, not v2.2 temporal migration, and not a lifecycle baseline freeze.

canonical_evidence_entries.plan_position: Phase E lifecycle-native sham-control hardware subset before task-benefit bridge
canonical_evidence_entries.claim: The five-profile lifecycle runtime executed the enabled lifecycle path and five predeclared sham-control modes on real SpiNNaker with exact expected summaries, no fallback, and preserved returned build/load/readback artifacts.
canonical_evidence_entries.caveat: Hardware sham-control subset only; not lifecycle task benefit, not autonomous lifecycle-to-learning MCPL, not speedup, not multi-chip scaling, not v2.2 temporal migration, and not a lifecycle baseline freeze.

canonical_evidence_entries.plan_position: Phase E lifecycle-native local task-benefit/resource bridge before hardware package
canonical_evidence_entries.claim: A bounded lifecycle-derived feature bridge locally separates the enabled lifecycle mode from sham controls on a compact delayed task and declares the resource/readback contract required before hardware packaging.
canonical_evidence_entries.caveat: Local contract/reference evidence only; not a hardware task-benefit pass, not autonomous lifecycle-to-learning MCPL, not multi-chip scaling, and not a lifecycle baseline freeze.

canonical_evidence_entries.tier_label: Tier 4.30g-hw - Lifecycle Task-Benefit / Resource Bridge Hardware
canonical_evidence_entries.plan_position: Phase E lifecycle-native hardware task-benefit/resource bridge before lifecycle-native baseline freeze
canonical_evidence_entries.claim: The five-profile lifecycle runtime connected enabled lifecycle state into a bounded task-bearing path on real SpiNNaker while five predeclared lifecycle controls closed the task gate, with returned resource/readback accounting and zero failed criteria.
canonical_evidence_entries.caveat: Hardware task-benefit/resource bridge only; host ferries the lifecycle gate into the task path. Not autonomous lifecycle-to-learning MCPL, not speedup, not multi-chip scaling, not v2.2 temporal migration, and not full organism autonomy.

next_steps: Start Tier 4.31a native temporal-substrate readiness only as a contract/readiness decision for the already-promoted v2.2 fading-memory software mechanism.
Do not move v2.2 temporal state to hardware without a separately predeclared local reference, source audit, and compact hardware smoke.
Continue toward lifecycle-to-learning MCPL only through a dedicated gate that removes the host-ferried bridge one interface at a time.
